name: Copy Editing
description: Evaluates the language quality, editorial consistency, and written clarity of the OER.
preamble: Copy editing for OER goes beyond surface-level grammar correction. It ensures that the resource communicates with precision, consistency, and accessibility across diverse academic audiences. Reviewers should read as a careful editor would — attending to the rhythm of prose, the logic of structure, and the clarity of every claim.

criteria.title: Grammar & Syntax
criteria.standard: Writing is grammatically correct throughout. Sentences are well-formed and syntactically clear.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Frequent grammatical errors, run-on sentences, or dangling modifiers disrupt comprehension.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Writing demonstrates mastery of grammar with sophisticated, varied sentence construction.

criteria.title: Spelling & Typographical Accuracy
criteria.standard: Text is free from spelling errors and typos. Technical terms are spelled correctly and consistently.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Multiple spelling errors or inconsistent spelling of key terms throughout the resource.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Zero errors. Technical vocabulary is deployed with precision and consistency across all sections.

criteria.title: Punctuation & Mechanics
criteria.standard: Punctuation is correctly and consistently applied throughout, including commas, apostrophes, and quotation marks.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Systematic punctuation errors (e.g., misused apostrophes, inconsistent serial comma) appear throughout.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Punctuation is not only correct but is used to create precise rhetorical effect and clarity.

criteria.title: Style Guide Consistency
criteria.standard: A consistent editorial style (APA, Chicago, or other) is applied throughout the resource.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Style is undefined or mixed across sections, creating confusion in citation and usage.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Style guide adherence is flawless and explicitly declared; all edge cases are handled consistently.

criteria.title: Internal Consistency & Usage
criteria.standard: Terminology, abbreviations, and naming conventions are used consistently throughout.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Key terms are used interchangeably or redefined across sections without explanation.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: A glossary or style sheet is evident. Terminology is introduced, defined, and applied with complete consistency.

criteria.title: Clarity & Readability
criteria.standard: Writing is clear and appropriate for college-level readers. Complex ideas are explained accessibly.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Jargon-heavy or overly dense prose impedes understanding without adequate scaffolding.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Exceptionally clear prose makes complex ideas accessible without sacrificing academic rigor.

criteria.title: Formatting & Visual Consistency
criteria.standard: Visual formatting (headings, lists, tables, fonts) is consistent and enhances readability.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Inconsistent fonts, random bolding, or misaligned formatting create a disorganized appearance.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Formatting is purposeful, consistent, and designed to support the learning hierarchy of the content.

criteria.title: Citations, References & Attributions
criteria.standard: All sources are properly cited and referenced according to the declared style guide.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Citations are missing, incomplete, or inconsistently formatted. Reference list does not match in-text citations.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: All citations are complete, accurate, and consistently formatted. DOIs and access dates are included where appropriate.

criteria.title: Cross-References & Navigational Elements
criteria.standard: Internal cross-references, tables of contents, and chapter links are accurate and functional.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Cross-references point to wrong sections. TOC entries are absent or inaccurate.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Navigation is comprehensive, accurate, and enhances the learning journey through clear wayfinding.

criteria.title: Inclusive & Accessible Language
criteria.standard: Language is gender-neutral, culturally sensitive, and free from stereotypes or bias.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Gendered or exclusionary language used without awareness. Examples center narrow demographic groups.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Language actively centers equity and inclusion. Diverse examples and perspectives are woven throughout.
